# Company Operations Handbook

## Onboarding New Employees

The onboarding process for a new employee should begin on their first day. New
employees are recommended to complete orientation, sign the code of conduct, and
meet their team. Managers should assign an onboarding buddy to every new hire so
onboarding stays consistent across teams.

## Company Policies

Company policies govern day-to-day conduct. The company travel policy is one of
several policies every employee should read. Policies are reviewed annually and
employees are recommended to acknowledge the current policies each year.

## Product Release Process

The product release process defines what a release candidate is. A release
candidate is a product build that has passed testing and is recommended for a
staged rollout. The product team should tag each product release candidate before
shipping.

## Support Ticket Escalation

Support tickets should be escalated when they exceed the service level agreement.
The support team is recommended to escalate any unresolved support ticket to a
senior engineer. Every support escalation should be logged.

## Vendor Contract Approval

Vendor contract approval follows the company policies for procurement. The product
owner role owns the decision to approve a new vendor contract, and legal should
review the policies before any vendor contract is signed.
